story_one_liner: While the world watches Hormuz, Israel is redrawing the map of southern Lebanon. Five framings of the same campaign — could every one be telling the truth?
voice_style: BBC-broadcaster cadence, grave, measured for humanitarian story
music_brief: ambient drone bed; intro sting handles open

world_tickers.headline: Japan to buy 20m UAE oil bypassing Hormuz
US trade gap widens on AI imports

scenes.voiceover: While the world watches Hormuz, Israel is redrawing the map of southern Lebanon. Quietly.
Three hundred and fifty thousand Lebanese have been forced from their homes. Eighty towns evacuated. Israel calls it a security zone. Five countries told this very differently.
Singapore's wire service kept it neutral. Four killed despite ceasefire. The mayor and his family.
scenes.headline_quoted: Israeli strikes on Lebanon kill 4 despite ceasefire.

scenes.voiceover: Saudi Arabia's Arab News used scare quotes. The plan, they said, would redraw the map.
scenes.headline_quoted: How Israel's 'buffer zone' plan could redraw the map of southern Lebanon.

scenes.voiceover: Egypt was sharper. Mada Masr's headline did not say security zone. It said occupying.
scenes.headline_quoted: Israel attacks villages north of Litani to ease pressure on troops occupying.

scenes.voiceover: And the Vatican stepped in. Pope Leo the Fourteenth video-called the priests of southern Lebanon. Personally.
scenes.headline_quoted: Their villages are being worn down by missiles and bombardments.

scenes.voiceover: And then, the line nobody else ran. Middle East Eye surfaced Israel's army chief in his own words.
scenes.headline_quoted: Israel's army chief sees a historic opportunity to reshape the region.

scenes.voiceover: Same campaign. Five framings. Could every one of them be telling the truth?
scenes.on_screen_text:
Same campaign.
Five framings.
Daily Framings — follow.

fact_check_provenance.primary_event_source: Lebanese Health Ministry + IDF statements + on-the-ground reporting from Mada Masr, Middle East Eye, CNA

supporting_quotes_per_frame.exact_headline: Israeli strikes on Lebanon kill 4 despite ceasefire
How Israel's 'buffer zone' plan could redraw the map of southern Lebanon
Israel attacks villages north of Litani to ease pressure on troops occupying crucial axis
Pope Leo XIV video-calls priests in southern Lebanon
Israel army chief sees 'historic opportunity' to reshape region

fact_check_provenance.note: Lower bucket count (7) than Hormuz/Turner but highest framing-divergence story of the day. Western mainstream press largely silent — itself part of the framing story (worth a newsletter callout, not the video itself).
